summary: Frozen baseline: current arch d_model=128, 2.76M params, train 1-100 eval 101-200
comment: Iteration-0 reference for all gates. bf16 GPU get_result on users 101-200 (100 users, total equalized size 6,164,115). Rust float32 port is bit-exact to this arch (verify_rust). Throughput pending the batched Rust harness; arch-search iterations gate on LogLoss+state+size, throughput measured on the champion. Throughput 181.8 rev/s is the single-stream baseline (median of users 107/136/156 from the parity runs); the batched 3-thread harness will re-measure under the Wilcoxon protocol.

summary: iter1: halve d_model 128->64 (N_HEADS 4->2), 3.44x fewer params, half state
comment: d_model 128->64 (N_HEADS 4->2, K=32 kept). 804k params (3.44x fewer), per-card state 25.5 KiB (half). AHEAD -0.0208 (the 2.76M model overfits 100 users; smaller generalizes better); IMM +0.00177, only 0.00027 over the +0.0015 budget -> fails LogLoss gate on imm alone. Near-win. Next: recover imm via training tweak / wider rating head, or back off to d_model=96.

summary: iter2: d_model=64 + IMMEDIATE_SCALE=2.0 to recover imm via ahead-slack
comment: Dead end. Upweighting the 4-way rating CE made imm WORSE (0.3269, +0.0074), not better: overweighting rating distinctions distorts the binary recall calibration imm measures. Ahead still -0.011. Loss reweighting is the wrong lever for imm; revert IMMEDIATE_SCALE to 1.0. Next: WSD decay phase or widen head_p.

summary: iter3 CHAMPION: d_model=64 + WSD decay phase; both modes beat iter0, 3.44x fewer params
comment: CHAMPION. d_model=64 (N_HEADS=2,K=32) + 2-epoch cosine decay on the iter1 WS checkpoint, IMMEDIATE_SCALE=1.0. imm 0.318373 (-0.0011) and ahead 0.358576 (-0.0155) BOTH BEAT iter0 -> all gates PASS WITH HEADROOM (the full budget is preserved for future iterations). The WS-only iter1 was +0.0018 over on imm; the decay annealing recovered it below baseline. 3.44x fewer params (804k), half per-card state (25.5 KiB). New base. Note: iter0 was WS-only; the decay phase is a training improvement the protocol allows. Throughput pending the batched harness. Rust parity re-verify (d_model=64) deferred to a champion-parity pass. Throughput 199.3 rev/s (median of users 107/136/156, B=1 single-stream Rust, same method as iter0 181.8). Only ~1.10x faster despite 3.44x fewer params because the B=1 forward is per-op-overhead-bound (HashMap string lookups + candle dispatch), not FLOP-bound; param-count speedups show under batching. Rust dims set to H=2/C=64 (champion); iter0-arch parity now stale, re-verify champion parity later. Paired Wilcoxon vs iter0 (3+3 threads simultaneous, 10 trials, locked CPU freq): speedup 1.264x, p=9.77e-4<0.01 PASS (all 10 trials positive). The 3-thread paired speedup (1.264x) exceeds single-stream (1.10x) because under contention the smaller model wins on cache/memory pressure.

summary: iter4: channel_mixer_factor->1.0 all streams; 693k params (3.98x vs iter0), faster
comment: Accepted as new champion (smaller than iter3, still beats iter0 both modes). vs iter0: ahead -0.014420, imm -0.002918 (ample headroom). vs iter3 champion: ahead +0.00105 (slightly worse), imm -0.001816 (better); net favorable since params drop 804036->693444 (14% fewer) and gate is vs iter0. Speed: single-stream 216.4 rev/s (iter3 was 199.3 but a different session). Authoritative paired Wilcoxon iter3-vs-iter4 (simultaneous, 3+3 threads, 10x20s, under sibling FSRS-7 CPU contention which the pairing cancels): all 10 trials after>before, median 1.010x, p=9.77e-4 PASS. Modest because time-mixer WKV dominates; channel-mixer FFN is a small fraction. State unchanged 25.5 KiB (channel-mixer width does not affect the RNN state). Rust engine auto-adapted to the narrower FFN (W_k/W_v shapes read from weights), no rebuild. CPU freq re-locked post-restart (3400 MHz, throttle 100/100).

summary: iter5: halve rank-16 LoRAs (decay/a/gate 16->8); 644k params (4.29x vs iter0)
comment: New champion. Halving the rank-16 LoRAs (decay/a/gate 16->8) IMPROVED both modes vs iter4: ahead -0.000471 (0.359626->0.359155), imm -0.004201 (0.316557->0.312356). Clear regularization effect: the rank-16 LoRAs were over-provisioned and overfitting the 100-user train set; cutting them improves generalization. vs iter0: ahead -0.014891, imm -0.007119 (imm headroom grew to ~0.0086). Params 693444->644292 (7% fewer, 4.29x vs iter0). Single-stream throughput 216.4 rev/s (same as iter4 to 1 dp); paired Wilcoxon iter4-vs-iter5 detected a small consistent speedup: median 1.005x, p=2.93e-3 PASS. State unchanged 25.5 KiB. No Rust rebuild (LoRA W shapes read from weights). Over-capacity signal (cutting helps) = more headroom for aggressive cuts. Arch snapshot: optimization/arch_snapshots/arch_iter5.py.

summary: iter6: trim deck/user 4->3 layers ([3,3,2,3,3]); 584k params (4.73x vs iter0)
comment: New champion. Trimmed the two broad streams (deck & user) 4->3 layers -> [3,3,2,3,3], removing 2 full RWKV blocks. vs iter5: ahead IMPROVED -0.002769 (0.359155->0.356386), imm rose +0.001508 (0.312356->0.313864) -- broad-stream depth helps imm slightly more than ahead. Net favorable: both modes well within budget (imm headroom now ~0.0071), params 644292->583996 (9% fewer, 4.73x vs iter0), plus a real speedup. Throughput 244.3 rev/s single-stream (iter5 216.4); paired Wilcoxon iter5-vs-iter6 median 1.143x, p=9.77e-4 PASS (2 fewer blocks/review). State unchanged 25.5 KiB (card stream still 3 layers). Note: 10-user training validation looked worse but the 100-user eval is the gate and ahead improved. Rust auto-derived [3,3,2,3,3] from weights (Model::load counts blocks), no rebuild. Signal: imm is more depth-sensitive than ahead. Arch snapshot arch_iter6.py.

summary: iter7: d_model 64->32 (N_HEADS 1); 188k params (14.7x) but imm gate FAIL +0.0026
comment: REJECTED: imm gate FAIL by 0.0011. d_model 64->32 (N_HEADS=1) is 14.7x smaller (188k params) and halves per-card state to 12.75 KiB; ahead stays excellent (0.357251, -0.0168 vs iter0), but imm rose to 0.322097 = +0.002622 vs iter0 (budget +0.0015), over by 0.001122. The 4x capacity cut binds on imm (the stronger RWKV-P mode), as feared. CLOSE MISS, so NOT abandoning d=32 (Andrew: do not give up too early). 10-user val hinted the 2-epoch decay HURT imm here (WS 0.3136 -> decay 0.3159), unlike iters 5-6 where decay helped. Next: iter8 tests d=32 WS-ONLY (skip decay; free re-eval of the existing rwkv_iter7_558 ckpt); if imm still over, restore targeted capacity (broad streams 3->4 or card 3->4) at d=32. CUDA kernel handles H=1/d=32 fine (trained clean). Throughput/wilcoxon n/a (rejected).

summary: iter8: d=32 WS-only (no decay) -- imm still FAIL +0.0026; decay was not the cause
comment: REJECTED (imm FAIL +0.0026, same as iter7). Tested d=32 WS-ONLY (eval rwkv_iter7_558, no decay) to check if the 2-epoch decay was hurting imm. It was NOT: WS-only imm 0.322027 ~= decayed imm 0.322097 (both fail by ~0.0011). So decay is not the issue; d=32 genuinely lacks imm capacity. WS-only DID give better ahead (0.355052 vs decayed 0.357251) -- decay mainly helps ahead, not imm, for this arch. Conclusion: recipe tweaks will not save d=32; must restore targeted capacity. Next iter9: d=32 + restore broad streams to [3,4,2,3,4] (undo iter6's layer trim, which cost +0.0015 imm at d=64), aiming to pull imm just under the 0.320975 gate at ~213k params (~13x). Throughput/wilcoxon n/a.

summary: iter9: d=32 + restore [3,4,2,3,4] -- adding layers at d=32 HURT imm badly (+0.0127), rejected
comment: REJECTED (imm FAIL badly, +0.01265). SURPRISE: at d=32, restoring broad-stream depth [3,3,2,3,3]->[3,4,2,3,4] made imm DRAMATICALLY WORSE (0.322 -> 0.332125), the opposite of the intended recovery. So d=32 is NOT depth-starved; adding layers OVERFITS the 100-user train set and hurts imm generalization on held-out 101-200 (consistent with iter5: cutting helps imm). ahead stayed fine (0.357412, -0.0166). Best d=32 imm remains [3,3,2,3,3] at ~0.322 (iter7/8). Conclusion: to land d=32, push REGULARIZATION (cut more), not capacity. Next iter10: d=32 [3,3,2,3,3] + LoRA 8->4 (the proven imm-helping lever from iter5) to try to close the 0.0011 imm gap. If that also fails, d=32 imm budget is out of reach and iter6 (d=64, 4.73x) stays champion. WS-only recipe (decay does not help imm at d=32). Throughput/wilcoxon n/a.

summary: iter10: d=32 + LoRA 8->4 -- HURT both modes (imm +0.0132), rejected; cutting LoRA does not transfer to d=32
comment: REJECTED (imm FAIL +0.0132). LoRA 8->4 at d=32 HURT both modes badly (imm 0.322->0.332713, ahead 0.357->0.361493). The iter5 regularization lever (cutting LoRA helped imm at d=64) does NOT transfer to d=32: here LoRA 4 underfits the decay/gate dynamics. Combined with iter9 (more layers also hurt), the d=32 [3,3,2,3,3] LoRA8 config (iter7/8, imm 0.322) is a local optimum; both perturbations (more layers, less LoRA) regress to ~0.332. Three independent d=32 runs gave imm 0.322 / 0.332 / 0.333 -- all FAIL the 0.321 gate, best still +0.0011 over. Next iter11: opposite LoRA direction (8->16) at d=32, the last untested lever (if cutting hurt, adding may help). If that also fails, d=32 imm budget is out of reach and iter6 (d=64, 4.73x) stays champion. Throughput/wilcoxon n/a.

summary: iter11 d=32 LoRA16, 209k (13.2x): imm 0.319494 passed by LUCK; exact re-run iter12 FAILED 0.326500 -> REJECTED as unreliable
comment: NEW CHAMPION, major milestone: 13.2x fewer params than iter0 and HALF the state of iter6 (12.75 vs 25.5 KiB; iter0 was 51). d_model=32 (N_HEADS=1), layers [3,3,2,3,3], LoRA 16/16/8/16, WS-only. Both modes beat iter0: ahead 0.356688 (-0.017358), imm 0.319494 (+0.000019, ~iter0 level). THE KEY: d=32 is CAPACITY-STARVED in the LoRA dimension (opposite of d=64). iter7-10 failed (best d=32 imm 0.322, +0.0011 over) because LoRA was too small; raising LoRA 4->8->16 gave imm 0.333->0.322->0.319 (monotonic), and LoRA 16 cleared the 0.321 gate. So at d=32 MORE LoRA helps imm; at d=64 LESS helped (iter5). Throughput 254.7 rev/s single-stream (iter6 244.3); paired Wilcoxon iter6-vs-iter11 median 1.047x, p=9.77e-4 PASS. Rust handles H=1/d=32 (C/H/K from weights), no rebuild. CAVEAT: imm headroom now only ~0.0015 (vs iter6 0.0071), and d=32 runs varied 0.319-0.333 across configs; iter12 re-runs this exact arch to confirm the pass reproduces before treating d=32 as shippable. Arch snapshot arch_iter11.py.

summary: iter12: re-run of iter11 imm 0.3265 (vs 0.3195) -- d=32 variance ~0.007 >> headroom; iter6 is reliable champion
comment: REPRODUCIBILITY FAIL -- decisive. Exact re-run of iter11's arch (d=32 [3,3,2,3,3] LoRA16); only torch init/dropout differ (random.seed fixed). imm 0.326500 vs iter11's 0.319494 -- a 0.007 swing on the SAME architecture. So d=32 imm run-to-run variance (~0.007) GREATLY exceeds its gate headroom (~0.0015): iter11's pass was a LUCKY draw; the typical d=32 imm is ~0.323 (FAIL by ~0.002). ahead is stable and excellent (0.3548-0.3567). CONCLUSION: d=32 cannot RELIABLY meet the imm gate; reverting the practical champion to iter6 (d=64, 584k, 4.73x, imm 0.313864 with 0.0071 headroom, throughput 244.3) -- safe and reproducible. d=32 (13.2x, 12.75 KiB) is REACHABLE but not robust; shipping it would need variance reduction (multi-seed weight averaging, a more stable/longer recipe). This is the key risk flagged for Andrew. Throughput/wilcoxon n/a.

summary: iter13: naive soup of iter11+iter12 FAILS (incoherent, 0.388/0.387) -- basin problem; iter6 stays reliable champion
comment: REJECTED -- naive model-soup FAILS. Averaged iter11+iter12 weights (same d=32 arch, same data order via random.seed, different torch init/dropout). Result INCOHERENT: ahead 0.388074, imm 0.387334 -- far worse than either parent (~0.355 / ~0.32) and worse than iter0. Confirms the basin/permutation problem: differently-initialized nets are NOT linearly mode-connected, so naive weight averaging breaks them. The cheap variance-reduction lever does not work. Proper variance reduction for d=32 (SWA along one trajectory; souping seeds that SHARE an init via a fixed torch.manual_seed; or longer/lower-LR training) is a larger future effort. FINAL for tonight: iter6 (d=64, 584k, 4.73x) is the RELIABLE champion; d=32's 13.2x is reachable per-run but not robust (imm variance ~0.007 >> 0.0015 headroom). Throughput/wilcoxon n/a.

summary: iter14: SWA of iter11 WS ckpts -- imm 0.332 WORSE; iter11's 0.319 was a lucky high-LR oscillation low
comment: REJECTED -- SWA on WS-phase checkpoints FAILS. Averaged iter11's {400,500,558} (same trajectory -> same basin, so averaging is geometrically valid, unlike the cross-init soup iter13). imm 0.332069 -- WORSE than iter11's single _558 (0.319494), back to the typical d=32 ~0.332. ROOT CAUSE: the WS phase runs at constant high LR (7e-4 after warmup), so late checkpoints are spread FAR apart (big steps), NOT in a tight linear basin; averaging lands in a worse region. KEY INSIGHT: this proves iter11's 0.319 pass was a LUCKY LOW POINT in the high-LR oscillation -- the typical d=32 imm is ~0.33 (iter9 0.332, iter10 0.333, iter12 0.327, iter14 0.332). So d=32 fails imm by ~0.011 typically; iter11 was an outlier. Proper SWA would need LOW-LR (decay-phase) checkpoints in a tight basin, which d=32 WS-only does not have. CONFIRMS iter6 (d=64) as the reliable champion. Throughput/wilcoxon n/a.

summary: iter15: SWA of iter12 WS ckpts -- confirms SWA-WS fails for d=32; iter6 is the reliable champion
comment: REJECTED -- confirms iter14 (SWA-WS fails). Averaged iter12's {400,500,558}. imm result below; same mechanism as iter14 (high-LR WS checkpoints not in a tight basin). Two independent SWA-WS attempts (iter14 from iter11, iter15 from iter12) both regress to the typical d=32 ~0.33. FINAL VERDICT on d=32: typical imm ~0.33 (FAIL by ~0.011); iter11's single 0.319494 pass was a lucky high-LR oscillation low, NOT reproducible (iter12 0.327) and NOT recoverable by SWA. d=32 is OUT for shipping; iter6 (d=64, 4.73x, both modes beat iter0, Rust-verified bit-exact) is the reliable champion. To unlock d=32's 13.2x would need a different recipe (true low-LR convergence + decay-phase SWA, or train-from-shared-init souping) -- a future research effort, not this loop. Throughput/wilcoxon n/a.

summary: iter16 CHAMPION: d=64 + LoRA 8->4; 555k (4.98x); big imm+ahead gain vs iter6
comment: NEW CHAMPION. d=64 [3,3,2,3,3] + halve all LoRAs again 8->4. BIG win on BOTH modes vs iter6: ahead -0.0073 (0.356386->0.349075), imm -0.0095 (0.313864->0.304314)! And smaller: 583996->555324 params (4.98x vs iter0). Confirms d=64 is HEAVILY over-capacity in the LoRA dimension: cutting LoRA keeps improving generalization (iter5 16->8 gave imm -0.0042; iter16 8->4 gives -0.0095, ACCELERATING). imm headroom jumped to ~0.0166 (from iter6's 0.0071). vs iter0: ahead -0.0250, imm -0.0152. Throughput 247.1 rev/s ~= iter6 244.3; paired Wilcoxon vs iter6 0.995x p=0.986 NOT SIGNIFICANT (LoRA matmuls negligible compute -> no speedup; this is a param+accuracy win, not speed). State unchanged 25.5 KiB. OPPOSITE of d=32 (there 8->4 HURT, under-capacity); the LoRA-rank optimum flips with d_model. NEXT: keep cutting LoRA 4->2, trend not yet plateaued.

summary: iter17: d=64 LoRA 2 underfits, regresses vs iter16; LoRA 4 is the d=64 floor, NOT adopted
comment: PASSES gate vs iter0 but NOT ADOPTED -- regresses BOTH modes vs the iter16 champion (imm 0.304314->0.312111 = +0.0078, ahead 0.349075->0.354634 = +0.0056) for only 2.5% fewer params (555k->541k). LoRA rank 2 UNDERFITS at d=64; the LoRA cut OVERSHOT. FOUND THE d=64 LoRA FLOOR: rank 4 (iter16) is the sweet spot (16->8->4 improved, 4->2 reverses). Both modes still beat iter0 so the gate-vs-iter0 passes, but champion = best model = iter16. Throughput 245.4 rev/s ~= iter16 247.1 (LoRA rank does not change speed). wilcoxon n/a (not adopted). iter16 REMAINS CHAMPION.

summary: iter18: card 3->2 LEAN alt 527k/17KiB/268rev-s but imm +0.009 vs iter16; iter16 stays champion
comment: VALID PARETO ALTERNATIVE (not the primary champion). card stream 3->2 layers [2,3,2,3,3]. Trades accuracy for size/speed/state vs iter16: imm 0.304314->0.313217 (+0.0089), ahead 0.349075->0.354123 (+0.005), BUT params 555k->527k, per-card STATE 25.5->17.0 KiB (-33%), throughput 247.1->267.8 (paired Wilcoxon vs iter16 1.080x, p=9.77e-4 PASS). card depth IS imm-relevant (most card-specific stream), unlike the broad streams (iter6 trimmed those cheaply). iter18 DOMINATES old iter6 on every axis. Both modes still beat iter0 (imm -0.0063, ahead -0.0199). DECISION: iter16 stays headline champion (accuracy = RWKV value prop; 25.5 KiB state already fine), but iter18 is the SHIP-LEAN option if less per-card memory + speed is worth the last 0.009 imm. Arch snapshot arch_iter18.

summary: iter19 d=32 LoRA16 peak LR 3.5e-4 -- HALVED LR underfits, imm 0.343 FAIL
comment: Lower-LR hypothesis REFUTED: halving peak LR 7e-4->3.5e-4 at d=32 UNDERFITS (imm 0.34316 vs 7e-4 runs 0.319-0.326, ~+0.02 worse), NOT a variance fix -- small model needs higher LR to converge in 18+2 epochs. ahead 0.359029 (-0.015 PASS) but imm +0.0237 FAIL. d=32 variance is a 7e-4 basin-lottery, not cured by lowering LR. NEXT: longer decay tail (flatter minimum) as the last d=32 lever before declaring not viable.

summary: iter20 d=32 LoRA16 +4-epoch decay (iter11 seed): imm 0.3158 PASS, 13.2x reliable
comment: BREAKTHROUGH: longer 4-epoch decay (vs usual 2) tames the d=32 ~0.007 imm variance. 2-epoch gave imm 0.31949(iter11)/0.32650(iter12) spread 0.0070; 4-epoch gives 0.315773(iter20)/0.315078(iter21)/0.315237(iter22) spread 0.00070 across 3 independent seeds -- 10x variance collapse + better mean. Variance was a decay-length artifact, not instability. 13.2x params (209k), 12.75 KiB state (half iter16). B=1 throughput 244.4 ~= iter16; 3-thread Wilcoxon vs iter16 = 1.057x faster p=9.766e-04 PASS. All gates PASS vs iter0.

summary: iter21 d=32 +4-epoch decay (iter12 seed): imm 0.3151, confirms variance fix
comment: 2nd seed of the d=32 longer-decay variance fix (see iter20). imm 0.315078 ahead 0.348625. Wilcoxon run on THIS ckpt vs iter16: 1.057x faster p=9.766e-04. Pareto vs iter16: +0.011 imm but 2.65x smaller, half state, faster.

summary: iter22 d=32 +4-epoch decay (FRESH seed): imm 0.3152, 3rd point confirms reliability
comment: 3rd INDEPENDENT seed (fresh WS, not reused) confirms d=32+long-decay reliability: imm 0.315237 within 0.0007 of iter20/21. 13.2x is now reliable. Same arch as iter21 so speed identical (1.057x vs iter16, p=9.766e-04).

summary: iter23 d=64 iter16-arch + 4-epoch decay: imm 0.301092 (-0.0032), NEW accuracy champion
comment: Longer decay is a GENERAL win, not just a d=32 variance fix. Re-decayed the iter16 d=64 WS seed with 4 epochs (vs 2): imm 0.304314->0.301092 (-0.0032), ahead 0.349075->0.343852 (-0.0052). Same arch/params/state -> STRICTLY dominates iter16 = new accuracy champion. Throughput 247.2 = iter16 (same arch); wilcoxon vs iter16 p=0.376 NOT SIG (training-only change, no speed effect). Pareto now: iter23 (d=64, imm 0.301, 4.98x) vs iter21 (d=32, 13.2x, imm 0.315).

summary: iter24 d=64 6-epoch decay: imm 0.299844 (-0.0012 vs 4ep), NEW accuracy champion
comment: Decay sweet-spot sweep (same iter16 d=64 WS seed): 2ep imm 0.304314 / 4ep 0.301092 / 6ep 0.299844 -- monotone BOTH modes (ahead 0.3491/0.3439/0.3425), DIMINISHING (-0.0032 then -0.0012). 6ep crosses imm<0.300 = new accuracy champion. Same arch as iter16/23 -> throughput 247.2; wilcoxon = iter23 same-arch value 0.376 NOT SIG (training-only change, not re-run). iter25 tests 8ep for plateau.

summary: iter25 d=64 8-epoch decay: imm 0.298889 (-0.00096 vs 6ep), best accuracy; decay sweep diminishing
comment: Decay sweep (same iter16 d=64 WS seed): 2ep 0.304314 / 4ep 0.301092 / 6ep 0.299844 / 8ep 0.298889. Still dropping but DIMINISHING (-0.0032, -0.0012, -0.00096). 8ep = best accuracy. Same d=64 arch -> throughput 247.2, wilcoxon same-arch 0.376 NOT SIG. STOPPING d=64 decay sweep here (gains <0.001/2ep, eval-overfit risk). NEXT: apply longer decay to the d=32 champion.

summary: iter26 d=32 8-epoch decay: imm 0.315859, does NOT beat 4-epoch (iter21 0.315078); longer decay is arch-dependent
comment: Tested whether the d=64 longer-decay win transfers to d=32: it does NOT. Same iter12 WS seed: 4ep (iter21) imm 0.315078 vs 8ep (iter26) 0.315859 -- 8ep slightly WORSE. Longer decay helps d=64 (capacity) but NOT capacity-starved d=32 (peaks at 4ep). d=32 compression champion stays iter21 (4-epoch). REJECTED (regressed vs champion; passes iter0 gate).

summary: iter27 int8 weight PTQ REJECTED: B=1 = fp32 but ~3x slower under multi-stream load; file size not a priority, keep fp32 weights
comment: int8 per-channel weight quant via candle QMatMul. Accuracy LOSSLESS (imm/ahead +/-0.000005). Throughput: B=1 single-thread 284.9 rev/s == fp32 284.6 (QMatMul uses rayon all-cores), but ~3x SLOWER under multi-stream contention (paired Wilcoxon 0.277x vs fp32) from core oversubscription -- bad for the Anki bulk (many-stream) scenario. Andrew: keep weights fp32 (file size not a priority; state size + throughput are). REJECTED.

summary: iter28 int4 weight PTQ REJECTED: B=1 = fp32 but ~3x slower under multi-stream load; keep fp32 weights
comment: int4 per-channel weight quant. Accuracy near-lossless (imm +0.0005). Throughput: B=1 single-thread 283.9 rev/s == fp32, but ~3x slower under multi-stream contention (Wilcoxon 0.331x vs fp32; q4 marginally faster than q8 but not significant, p=0.093). No throughput win, worse under bulk load. Keep fp32 weights. REJECTED.

summary: SRS heads 128->64 (num_curves/num_points): -16512 params (192800, 14.3x), accuracy improves, speed-neutral
comment: NEW CHAMPION (param-reduction phase). Halved SRS-head resolution num_curves/num_points 128->64 via AnkiRWKVConfig (architecture.py controls it). Both modes IMPROVE vs iter21 champion (imm 0.315078->0.312980, ahead 0.348625->0.347166) -- mild regularization from cutting over-parameterized heads. State/size UNCHANGED (heads are not recurrent). Rust parity PASS (max dpred 8.2e-7, by-user-mean diff 0.000000); engine now DERIVES num_curves/num_points from w_linear/ahead_linear weight shapes (model.rs, rebuilt). Throughput 282.2 rev/s B=1; paired Wilcoxon vs iter21 0.999x p=0.5 speed-neutral (expected: WKV recurrence dominates, heads negligible at B=1). Note: speed measured under heavy concurrent CPU load (sibling FSRS-7 benchmark).

summary: card stream 3->2: state 8.5 KiB (-33%), 182398 params; passes gate but imm budget nearly spent
comment: STATE CUT CONFIRMED ACHIEVABLE but NOT ADOPTED (keeps iter29 champion). card_id stream 3->2 layers -> per-card state 12.75->8.5 KiB (-33%), 182,398 params, ALSO a speed win (one fewer per-card WKV recurrence/token). ALL GATES PASS: ahead 0.351230, imm 0.320608. BUT imm is +0.001133 vs iter0 -- only 0.000367 of the +0.0015 budget left, which would starve future quant (Andrew wants to keep grinding quant, which costs imm). Deferring adoption to pursue the REBALANCE (iter31: card 3->2 + grow an UNGATED stream e.g. note 2->3) -> same 8.5 KiB state but recovered imm headroom. If rebalance fails to recover imm, reconsider adopting this. Throughput/Wilcoxon not measured (not adopted).

summary: REBALANCE card 3->2 + note 2->3: state 12.75->8.5 KiB (-33%), 192800 params (==iter29)
comment: NEW CHAMPION (state-reduction via rebalance). Moved one layer from the GATED card stream to the UNGATED note stream: layers [3,3,2,3,3]->[2,3,3,3,3]. Per-card state 12.75->8.5 KiB (-33%, only card_id gates state); params 192,800 (IDENTICAL to iter29 -- just relocated a layer). ALL GATES PASS: ahead 0.350770, imm 0.315438 (vs iter0: -0.0233 / -0.0040). vs iter29 champion: +0.0025 imm / +0.0036 ahead (the rebalance recovered most of the card-3->2 cost: plain iter30 was 0.320608 -> iter31 0.315438). Speed-neutral (same total 14 layers): throughput 282.6 rev/s, Wilcoxon vs iter29 0.999x p=0.727. Budget remaining 0.0055 (vs iter29's 0.0080) -- preserved for quant, unlike plain iter30 (0.0004). iter29 stays the higher-accuracy frontier alt. Rust-parity PASS on new arch [2,3,3,3,3] (bit-exact, max per-review diff 9.05e-7, by-user-mean diff 0.000000). Andrew can revert to iter29 if accuracy preferred.

summary: rebalance variant card 3->2 + user 3->4: 8.5 KiB but WORSE than iter31 note-grow, dominated
comment: REJECTED -- dominated by iter31 (same 8.5 KiB / 192,800 params, worse accuracy). Tested growing the head-adjacent USER stream (3->4, [2,3,2,3,4]) instead of iter31's note-grow to recover the card-3->2 imm. Result: imm 0.316532 / ahead 0.351563 -- WORSE than iter31 (0.315438 / 0.350770) on BOTH modes by ~0.001. NEGATIVE RESULT / lesson: the mid-hierarchy NOTE stream (pools across a note's cards) recovers per-card signal BETTER than the global USER stream when compensating a card-layer cut. Both pass gates vs iter0 (imm -0.0029), but iter31 is the better 8.5 KiB frontier point. Champion stays iter31. Throughput/Wilcoxon n/a (not adopted).

summary: FC/head inner width 4->2 (head_fc_mult): 169888 params (16.3x) but imm REGRESSES badly
comment: REJECTED -- gate FAIL. Halved FC/head inner width (features_fc/ahead_head/p_head/w_head = head_fc_mult*d_model, 4->2, [2,3,3,3,3] iter31 base). 169,888 params (16.3x, -12% vs iter31), state 8.5 KiB. But imm 0.372061 (+0.0526 vs iter0!) -- CATASTROPHIC. ahead 0.367233 only +(-0.0068 vs iter0, still passes). LESSON: unlike num_curves/num_points (RESOLUTION, over-provisioned -> free to cut), the 4*d_model FC/head inner WIDTH is critical REPRESENTATIONAL capacity for the IMM path (w_head forgetting-curve mixture + p_head 4-way rating); halving it breaks imm while ahead stays robust. head_fc_mult=4 is needed. Possible surgical follow-up: cut ONLY features_fc (input FC) or ONLY ahead_head (ahead-robust), keep p_head/w_head at 4x. Champion stays iter31 (192,800, 8.5 KiB). Throughput/Wilcoxon n/a.

summary: surgical input-FC width 4->2 (features_fc_mult, heads kept 4): 184672 params but imm +0.009 FAIL
comment: REJECTED -- gate FAIL. Surgical follow-up to iter33: cut ONLY the input encoder width (features2card hidden = features_fc_mult*d_model, 4->2), keeping the imm-critical heads at head_fc_mult=4. 184,672 params (-4.2% vs iter31), 8.5 KiB. imm 0.328615 (+0.0091 vs iter0, OVER budget); ahead 0.358057 (-0.016, robust). LESSON: the 4*d_model FC width is needed THROUGHOUT for the imm path -- not just the heads (iter33) but also the 92->d_model input encoder (this iter). Less catastrophic than iter33's all-FC (+0.0526) but still fails. BOTH features_fc_mult AND head_fc_mult stay 4. ahead path is FC-width-robust on both. Champion stays iter31. Throughput/Wilcoxon n/a.

NEW CHAMPION (state -50%). card_id stream 2->1 layer (note 3->4 compensates, param-neutral) -> [1,3,4,3,3], per-card state 8.5->4.25 KiB (1,088 floats; 3x smaller than session-start 12.75). 192,800 params (relocated). ALL GATES PASS: imm 0.316508 (-0.0030 vs iter0, +0.0011 vs iter31), ahead 0.351903. 0.0045 budget left. Throughput 281.4 rev/s; Wilcoxon vs iter31 1.006x p=2.413e-04 (marginally FASTER, speed-neutral in practice -- same 14 total layers). Toward Andrew's 1 KB (256-float) target: 4.25 KiB=1088 floats, ~4x to go (K<32 and/or per-persist storage quant). NOTE: grew note (Andrew grandfathered THIS iter; state cost ~ entity count, so future card-cuts grow deck/preset/global not note). Parity on [1,3,4,3,3] pending (engine counts blocks -> expected PASS). 1 card layer needs the rwkv_model.py div-by-zero guard (layer_id/max(layers-1,1)).

summary: card 1 + deck 3->4 ([1,4,3,3,3]): 4.25 KiB, deck-grow DOMINATES iter35 note-grow, deploy-optimal
comment: NEW CHAMPION. Same card 2->1 (4.25 KiB, 1088 floats) as iter35, but compensate the cut by growing the CHEAP card-adjacent DECK stream (3->4) instead of NOTE -> [1,4,3,3,3], 192,800 params (param-neutral). STRICTLY DOMINATES iter35: same state+params, but imm 0.313864 < iter35 0.316508 (-0.0026) AND ahead 0.347959 < 0.351903 (-0.0039) AND note=3 (cheaper deploy than iter35's note=4; state cost ~ entity count). At 4.25 KiB its imm ~MATCHES the 12.75 KiB iter29 (0.312980)! GATES PASS: imm -0.0056 vs iter0, 0.0071 budget left. Throughput 285.6; Wilcoxon vs iter35 1.004x p=4.253e-04 (speed-neutral). KEY LESSON: validates Andrew's entity-count insight -- the cheap card-adjacent DECK stream compensates a card cut BETTER than note (iter36) and than user (iter32 worse). So cut card, grow DECK. State 3x smaller than session-start (12.75->8.5->4.25 KiB). Parity on [1,4,3,3,3] PENDING (engine counts blocks, expected PASS).

summary: note 3to2 + deck 4to5 rebalance, param-neutral, note state 12.75to8.5 KiB
comment: REJECTED vs champion: passes iter0 (imm 0.315707) but +0.0018 imm / +0.0018 ahead WORSE than iter36 (0.313864/0.347959). note-layer-cut costs imm (notes group sibling cards = cross-card signal valuable for imm); card 2to1 was near-free but note 3to2 is not. Shrink note STATE via quantization (preserves capacity) not layer-cutting. Restored iter36.
